ShipToNum.description: Contains the key of the default ship to for the customer. A blank value indicates that the name and address in the Customer file is considered the default ship to. This field is updated when the user marks the check box in ship to maintenance indicating that the ship to is to be designated as the default. This default will be used in areas such as Sales Order entry.

TermsCode.description:
The Terms.TermsCode value of the default sales terms associated with the customer. A default may be supplied by XaSyst.TermsCode if not blank. The terms will default into quotes and orders for this customer.
For invoices not related to a sales order, these terms will also default into the invoice.

ShipViaCode.description: Contains the ShipVia.ShipViaCode value of the default ShipVia for the customer.

PrintStatements.description: Controls whether or not this customer's statement will print when   printing of customer statements.

PrintLabels.description: Only customers that are PrintLabels = Yes will be selected for printing of mailing labels.

PrintAck.description:
Allows the user to establish whether or not a specific customer requires Sales Order Acknowledgements.  This does not force or limit the printing of sales acknowledgments directly from within Order Entry.  Order entry displays this setting to the user so that they know if they should print the acknowledgment.
For batch mode printing, (where ranges of sales orders are selected...future release) this setting will be used to exclude orders from printing.

FinCharges.description: Controls whether or not the customer will be included in the finance charge calculation process.

CreditHold.description: Indicates if customer has been placed into a "Credit Hold" status. A "yes" will trigger notification of this condition in Order Entry and Shipping.

GroupCode.description: Contains the CustGrup.GroupCode value of the customer group that the customer has been assigned to. This field is used by the application for sorting or filtering on reports and can also be associated with price lists.

DiscountPercent.description: An optional field used to establish a default purchasing discount percentage for any order placed by customer. This value is supplied to order entry as a default for line item discount percent.

PrimPCon.description: Contains the CustCnt.ConNum value of the Primary Purchasing contact for the customer. This field is not directly maintainable. Instead it is set during contact maintenance by having the user mark a check box indicating primary Purchaser.  This is the contact that is used as a default in Order Entry and Quoting.

PrimBCon.description: The same as the PrimPCon except that this is the Primary Billing Contact and this is used as a default in invoice entry.

PrimSCon.description: Same as PrimPCon except that this the Primary Shipping Contact and is used as a default in Packing Slip entry.

Comment.description:
Comments are intended to be internal comments about a specific customer. These do get pulled into other programs. They are mainly intended as an online storage facility.
To be view-as EDITOR widget.

EstDate.description: The date when the customer was established as a customer. Use the system date as a default when creating new customers.

FaxNum.description: The Fax Number for the customer. Optional field. Field is displayed in Order entry when no contact is specifically given or the contact has a blank fax number.

PhoneNum.description: The general Business Phone Number for the customer. Displayed in Order entry when no contact is given or when contact has a blank phone number.

TaxExempt.description: Indicates the reason why the customer is normally exempt from sales tax. Used as a default in invoice entry.  If field is non-blank it is considered exempt.

MarkUpID.description: The QMarkup.MarkupID value of the quote markup table which will be used to provide default markup percents in quote entry for this customer. If left blank the quote module will use the default quote markup table for the customer. (See EQSyst.MarkupID).

BillDay.description:
Represents the day of the week or month that this customer is invoiced on. The possible choices are determined by the Customer.Bill-Frequency field value.

When the Bill-Frequency is "W" (Weeky):
The valid values are 0-7 where 0=All Days,1=Sun,2=Mon,...,7=Sat.  

ll-frequency is 'M' (Monthly) this field contains the 1st -> 31st as possible choices to represent the day of the month to bill on.

OneInvPerPS.description: Determines whether or not packing slips for the same Sales Order and Fiscal Period will combined into a single invoice or not. If the packing slips are for different sales orders for the customer or fall in different fiscal periods, seperate invoices will be created even when this field is set to Yes.

DefaultFOB.description: Contains the default FOB.FOB value of the FOB policy for this  customers orders.  Default used in sales order entry for this customer.

CreditIncludeOrders.description: Determines whether or not Open Sales Orders are to be included in the credit limit checking process for the customer. This checkbox will also include open service contracts.

CreditReviewDate.description: Date on which the next credit review should be conducted for the customer.

CreditHoldDate.description: Date on which the customer was last placed on credit hold. This field is maintained by the system.

CreditHoldSource.description: Indicates how the customer was placed on credit hold.  Valid values are "MANUAL", "INVOICES", "ORDERS", and "CONTRACTS".  "MANUAL" means that the user placed the customer on hold.  INVOICES means that the customer's open A/R balance exceeded the credit limit.  ORDERS means that the sum of the open A/R and the open orders exceeded the credit limit. This field is maintained by the system.

CreditClearUserID.description: The UserFile.DCDUSERID value of the user that last cleared the customer's credit hold. This field is maintained by the system.

CreditClearDate.description: The date that the user last cleared the customer's credit hold. This field is maintained by the system.

CreditClearTime.description: The time that the user last cleared the customer's credit hold in HH:MM:SS format. This field is maintained by the system.

EDICode.description: The Trading Partner ID that is used for incoming and outgoing EDI transactions.

CurrencyCode.description: Contains the Currency.CurrencyCode value of the customer's base currency.

CountryNum.description: Contains the Country.CountryNum value of the country the customer is located in.

LangNameID.description: Contains the LangName.LangNameID value of the customer's language. This controls which language will be selected when extracting part descriptions from PartLangDesc table and report labels for customer related forms such as orders, packing slips and invoices.

BorderCrossing.description: Area/City code from where goods cross the border. This field is intended for Intrastat reporting. The field can be blank to indicate the value from the Country table.  This field is only visible if ISSyst.EnableHarbour is set to yes.

FormatStr.description: Optional custom address format used to format the customer's main address.

BTName.description: The Bill To name of this customer. Will be used by the AR module for Invoices. This defaults to the Customer.Name but can be overrode by the user.

BTAddress1.description: The first line of the customer's Bill To address.

BTAddress2.description: The second line of the customer's Bill To address.

BTAddress3.description: The second line of the customer's Bill To address.

BTCity.description: The city portion of the customer's Bill To address.

BTState.description: The state or province portion of the customer's Bill To address.

BTZip.description: The zip or postal code portion of the customer's Bill To address.

BTCountryNum.description: The Country.Countrynum value of the Country portion of the customer's Bill To address.

BTCountry.description: Contains the Country.Description value of the Country portion of the customer's Bill To address.

BTPhoneNum.description: The phone number related to the customer's Bill To Address.

BTFaxNum.description: The fax number of the customer's Bill To address.

BTFormatStr.description: Optional custom address format used to format the customer's Bill To address.

ParentCustNum.description: The Customer.CustNum value of the customer's parent company.

TaxRegionCode.description: Contains the TaxRgn.TaxRegionCode value of the customer's tax region for purposes of Sales Tax calculations.

ICCust.description: Determines whether or not this customer is an inter-company customer.

ContBillDay.description: The day of the month that service contracts for the customer marked for recurring invoicing are billed on.  If the invoice group's invoice date is greater than or equal to this date then the invoice will be generated.

EMailAddress.description: Default email address for the customer.

ShippingQualifier.description: Determines whether or not the customer will accept partial shipments at the line or order level. This functionality is only available with the Advanced Material Management module.

AllocPriorityCode.description: Contains the AllocPri.PriorityCode value of the priority that this customer's orders receive. This functionality is only available with the Advanced Material Management module.

ReservePriorityCode.description: Contains the ReservePri.PriorityCode value of the priority that this customer's orders receive. This functionality is only available with the Advanced Material Management module.

WebCustomer.description: Indicates if this customer will be able to access Customer Connect.  Only Customers with this equal to YES will be synchronized between the Manufacturing System DB and Customer Connect DB.

CustomerType.description: Used to define the type of the customer record.

NoContact.description: Indicates whether or not this customer will be included in marketing lists.

TerritoryLock.description: Determines whether or not the customer's territory can be changed by system processes that could potentially change the territory from its current value.

PendingTerritoryID.description: The pending sales territory that the customer will be assigned to based on changes to the territory boundaries.  This functionality is only available with the CRM module.

ConsolidateSO.description: Determines whether or not shipments to this customer for different sales orders within the same fiscal period wil be consolidated into one invoice. (See Customer.OneInvPerPS - for the shipments from the same sales order are handled).

CreditIncludePI.description: Indicates that Payment Instruments (bank drafts, post dated checks) are to be included in the credit limit checking.

GlobalCust.description: Determines whether or not this customer is shared between more than one company.

ICTrader.description: Indicates if this customer participates in the Inter-Company Trading.

TaxAuthorityCode.description: Establishes the tax authority for this customer.

ExternalDeliveryNote.description: Determines whether or not an external delivery note is required for the customer.  This field is available only when ExtCompany.SendShip is set to Yes.  This will provide the default for the ShipHead record.

GlobalCredIncOrd.description: Determines whether or not Open Orders are to be included in the global credit limit checking process. This checkbox will also include open service contracts.

GlobalCredIncPI.description: Indicates whether or not Payment Instruments (bank drafts, post dated checks) are to be included in the credit limit checking process.

GlobalCurrencyCode.description: Holds the Currency.CurrencyCode value of that the global customer will exchange data in.

GlobalCreditHold.description: Determines whether or not the customer has been placed into a "Global Credit Hold" status.  Any non-blank value will trigger notification of this condition in Order Entry and Shipping.

GlobalLock.description: Determines whether or not this customer record will receive global updates.

CheckDuplicatePO.description: Determines whether or not the system should check existing orders for this customer to insure that the same PO number is not used twice by the customer.

CreditLimit.description: An optional field that allows user to enter a monetary value to be used as a Credit limit.  A credit limit of zero is considered as having unlimited credit.

CustPILimit.description: An optional field that allows user to enter a monetary value to be used as a credit limit for payment instruments such as post dated checks or bank drafts.  A credit limit of zero is considered as having unlimited credit.

GlobalCreditLimit.description: An optional field that allows user to enter a monetary value to be used as a Global Credit Limit.  A credit limit of zero is considered as having unlimited credit.

GlobalPILimit.description: An optional field that allows user to enter a monetary value to be used as a credit limit for payment instruments such as post dated checks or bank drafts.  A credit limit of zero is considered as having unlimited credit.

DocGlobalCreditLimit.description: An optional field that allows user to enter a monetary value to be used as a Global Credit limit.  Credit limit of zero is considered as having unlimited credit. Stored in Global Currency.

DocGlobalPILimit.description: An optional field that allows user to enter a monetary value to be used as a Credit limit for payment instruments such as post dated checks or bank drafts.  Credit limit of zero is considered as having unlimited credit. Stored in Global currency

RfqAttachAllow.description: Indicates whether RFQ Attachments are allowed for this Customer

DiscountQualifier.description:
The discount qualifier is primarily used when applying order value based discounts to the customer's sales orders.  The value of this field affects the discount percent given to the customer.  Here's the rule:
"MIN" = means that the default order discount percent is the minimum discount the customer could get as compared to the order value based discount.
"MAX" = means that the default order discount percent is the maximum discount the customer could get as compared to the order value based discount.
"ADD" = means that the customer could get the order value based discount in addition to the default order discount.

AllowAltBillTo.description: Specifies the current customer can be an alternate bill to customer.

DemandDeliveryDays.description: Days to use in calculating the Order Detail Ship By date from the incoming need by date.

DemandDateType.description:
Indicates incoming date type.  Values are:
S - Shipping Date
N - Need By Date

DemandAddLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when adding a new order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandAddAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the add lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandChangeLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when changing an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.  This does not include changes to quantity or dates.

DemandChangeAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the change lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandCancelLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when canceling an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandCancelAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the cancel lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandNewLineLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when adding a new order line record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandNewLineAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the new line lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandQtyChangeLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when changing the quantity on an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandQtyChangeAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the quantity change lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandChangeDateLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when changing the date on an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandChangeDateAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the change date lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

CODCheck.description: Cashier's Check or Money order is required on COD Delivery

CODAmount.description: Amount due on Cashier's check or money order

GroundType.description: Valid Values are blank, "Any" (Any Payment), "GF" (Guaranteed Funds), or "Cash" (Currency)

NotifyFlag.description: Indicates whether to send an email notification of delivery

NotifyEMail.description: The list of email address to notify about a delivery

DeclaredIns.description: Flag to indicate that an insurance value was declared on delivery

PeriodicityCode.description: Periodicity Code.  Must be a valid code in the Periodicity table.

ServRelease.description: Service Signature release is on file

EarlyBuffer.description: Used to calculate on-time delivery performance rating

LateBuffer.description: Used to calculate on-time delivery performance rating

DemandUnitPriceDiff.description: Indicates if the unit price between the demand and the contract should be validated.  If this flag is checked, and the prices are different, when the demand is accepted a record will be written to the DemandLog table.

DemandUnitPriceDiffAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the unit price between the demand and the contract is different.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the unit price is different but allow the record to be accepted).

ExcFromVal.description: A flag that indicates whether this address should be validated by the tax service.

AddressVal.description: A flag indicating that an address has already been validated. This helps improve the performance of the bulk address validation process by allowing address that have already been validated to be skipped. This flag is set anytime a successful validation is performed, either by the bulk address validation or validation from the Customer form.

RebateVendorNum.description: This is the Vendor ID to be used when generating a Rebate for the customer

RebateForm.description: Indicates if the rebate should be a Check or a Credit Memo

CreditCardOrder.description: Indicates if the order should default as a credit card order.  Can be overriden at the order level.

DemandCheckForPart.description: Check for the part in the Part master.

DemandCheckForPartAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the Check for Part options is selected and the part is not in the part master file.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the part is not in the part master but allow the record to be accepted).

ChangedBy.description: Userid of user who made the last change to this record.

ChangeDate.description: The date that the record was last changed

ChangeTime.description: The time that the record was last change (seconds since midnight)

ChargeCode.description: Unique Identifier for the Finance Charges

IndividualPackIDs.description: Individual Pack ID'srequired. Used in manifesting.

IntrntlShip.description: International Shipment flag. Used in manifesting.

CertOfOrigin.description: Certificates of Origin required flag. Used in manifesting.

CommercialInvoice.description: Commercial Invoice required flag. Used in manifesting.

ShipExprtDeclartn.description: Ship Export Declaration required flag. Used in manifesting.

LetterOfInstr.description: Letter of Instruction required. Used in manifesting.

FFCity.description: Freight Forwarder city portion of address.

FFState.description: Freight Forwarder state portion of address.

FFZip.description: Freight Forwarder postal code or zip code portion of address.

FFCountry.description: Freight Forwarder country portion of address.

FFCountryNum.description: Freight Forwarder country number portion of address.

NonStdPkg.description: Non Standard Package flag. Used in manifesting.

DeliveryConf.description:
Determines the level of delivery confirmation.
1 - No Signature Required
2 - Adult Signature Required
3 - Confirmation Required
4 - Verbal Confirmation Required

AddlHdlgFlag.description: Additional Handling flag. Used in manifesting.

UPSQVEmailType.description: Always equal to Customer.  Used to link the customer table to the UPSQVEmail table.

ETCAddrChg.description: Used to determine if an address changed because of the tax integration.  If true, the tax integration changed the address.

TaxRoundRule.description: Define the rule how Tax amount from for sales related documents shall be rounded to the general number of decimals from the Currency:  0 - Use rounding rules from Currency; 1 - Round up; 2 - Round down; 3 - Normal rounding.  Used when Company parameter - Customer specific Tax Rounding is set.

TaxMethod.description: Tax calculation per line or invoice.  It can be "L" = per line or "I" = per invoice.  Default is "L".

ValidPayer.description: Remit cash receipts in AR. No cash receipts will be accepted from customers without this authorization

ValidSoldTo.description: Sold to customer in the AR Invoice Entry. The user will not be able to enter invoices for customers without this authorization

ValidShipTo.description: Receive shipments for orders entered in OE. Unless the user selects this option, the user must enter a Ship-to address in the Order Entry form.

OverrideRlsClass.description: Children are allowed to pay for their parents

AcrossNatAcc.description: Accept payments from any customer within the national account, regardless of parent-child status

NAParentCreditIsUsed.description: allow use Parent Credit in National Account

NACreditIsShare.description: allow/deny to a customer share his own credit with other customers within the National account

NACreditPreferenceList.description: define what type of credit will be used first when the customer

NAParentCreditPrc.description: Max Percent of Parent Credit to Use

NACreditSharedPrc.description: Percentage of the customer credit shared to his Children.

AllowOTS.description: Indicates if One Time Shipto information can be entered for this customer.  OTS, are entered in Quote, Sales Order, RMA, Service Contract and Service Call. In Shipping and Invoicing the OTS infomation is used.

ThirdPLCust.description: Flag indicating that this is a 3PL customer.

ManagedVendID.description: VendID of the associated supplier.  Only populated if ManagedCust flag = true.  Entered through Supplier Entry

ManagedVendNum.description: VendNum associated with VendID of associated supplier.  Only populated if ManagedCust flag = true.

DirectDebiting.description: Direct Debiting flag for use with Promissory Notes.

ReminderCode.description: Unique Identifier for the Reminder Group.

GlbNAParentCreditIsUsed.description: allow use Global Parent Credit in National Account

GlbNACreditIsShare.description: allow/deny to a customer share his own credit with other customers within the Global National account

GlbNAParentCreditPrc.description: Max Percent of Global Parent Credit to Use

GlbNACreditSharedPrc.description: Percentage of the customer credit shared to his Global Children.

AllowShipTo3.description: When yes, a ShipTo CustID on certain forms will be enabled. This allows a shipto of a different customer to be referenced as a 3rd party for a document.

OTSSaveAs.description: The default value used on One Time Ship To panels. Valid values blank = None, C = Customer, P = Prospect, S = Suspect T = Ship To

CustPartOpts.description:
Determines print options for any customer parts related to this customer.

M = Main Part Reference (Default)
S = Secondary Part Reference
O = Only Part Reference Printed
N = Customer Part Not Printed

HasBank.description: If yes, indicates that Customer has at least one associated CustBank record.

PMUID.description: Unique identifier of the payment method

ShipToTerrList.description: List of all ship to territories for the customer

AcctRefNumber.description: The Banking Reference number to be used on AR Invoice sent to the customer

InvPerPackLine.description: If this flag it?s turned on then one invoice per packing line will be created when invoicing a packing slip at the ?Get Shipments? action on the AR Invoice entry.

DemandCloseRejSkd.description: Define if at the moment of processing a demand the process should also close those rejected schedules that remain at demand entry

DemandCloseNoMatch.description: Flag that indicates if the Demand non matched will be closed.

DmdCheckShipAction.description: Flag to Check Partial Shipments Action B = Stop W = Warning

DemandCheckCUMM.description: Flag to check for cummulative info.

DemandCheckCUMMAction.description: Flag to Check what action should the system take the options are B = Stop and  W Warning

DemandPricing.description: Defines if Internal Pricing or Customer Pricing will be used for checking price differences

PriceTolerance.description: Defines the tolerance for price difference validations

PreferredBank.description: Preferred bank for cash receipts from sale to customer.

CheckDateCapPromise.description: If this flags is turned on then Demand CTP will automatically be executed as a part of the demand to order process.

CheckConfirmCapPromise.description: Confirm or not the Capable to Promise jobs from Demand Entry

CheckUpdateCapPromise.description: If checked, Updates the date in Demand Entry

DemandCapPromiseDate.description: This field will define the dates that will be validated as a part of the demand to order process. The valid values for this combo will be:  Need By (N), Ship By (S) and Both (B)

DemandCapPromiseAction.description: The value on this field will define the action to be taken when validating CTP. The valid values for this combo box will be: Warning (W) and Stop (S)

DemandCapPromiseUpdate.description:
This field will define the dates that will be updated as a part of the demand to order process. The valid options will be:
Need By (N), Ship By (S) and Both  (B) NOTE .-In all the cases above the update of date will only be done if the CTP dates are beyond the dates on the file.

DueDateCriteria.description: Criteria used for the calculation of the due date. Only enable if Periodic Billing status is active.

PBTerms.description: Periodic Billing Terms. A period between the billing or summarizing date and the due date (for example: 30 days, 45 days, 60 days).

ERSOrder.description: Evaluated Receipt Settlement Order. This flag will be used as a default when the user creates a Demand Entry PO or a Sales Order

DemandSplitSched.description: This checkbox indicates if the Demand schedule could be splited when there are not enough stock for a part, from the CTP.

OTSmartString.description: When set to TRUE the smart string functionality will only be processed when the incoming demand is new. After it has been processed and saved, if a retransmission is sent the smart string values will be ignored.

DeferredRev.description: Indicates if revenue is deferred for contracts assigned to this group.

DemandCheckConfig.description: If true then demand will be rejected when one or more demand lines are not configured properly. Applies only to Configurable parts.

DemandCheckCfgAction.description: Indicates the action to be taken if configuration values have not been entered for one or more demand lines.

SysRevID.description: Revision identifier for this row. It is incremented upon each write.

SysRowID.description: Unique identifier for this row. The value is a GUID.

PEIdentityDocType.description: Displays list of Peru Identity Types.

PENoAddress.description: Peru No Address Provided withholding status.

DemandCheckForRunOutPart.description: Check if the part is a run out part.

DemandCheckForRunOutPartAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the Check for Run Out Part option is selected and the part is marked as a run out part.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the part is marked as run out but allow the record to be accepted).

COOneTimeID.description: Colombia Loc Field. OneTimeCustVend new table ID

ECCType.description: Type of customer created by ECC.  Valid values are: B=B2b, C=B2C

SyncToExternalCRM.description: This field defines if the customer is synchronized to an External CRM. Only enabled if the External CRM integration is on at the Company configuration.

ExternalCRMCustomerID.description: This field holds the id of this customer in the External CRM

ExternalCRMCustomerType.description: This fields holds the customer type of this customer in the External CRM.

ExternalCRMLastSync.description: This field defines the last time that the  Customer  has been Synchronized between Epicor ERP and the External CRM. This field is maintained by the External CRM Synchronization  process.

ExternalCRMSyncRequired.description: This fields determines if the customer needs to be synchronized to the External CRM. If there are changes in the customer master file , Epicor ERP automatically turns on this field.

NumberOfEmployees.description: Number of employees of the customer

TickerLocation.description: Location of the ticker of this customer

ConsolidateLinesPerPart.description: Indicates that the shipment lines will be consolidated within the invoice for the same part, the same order, and the same packing slip.

INGSTComplianceRate.description: GST Compliance Rate field for India

SendToFSA.description: Determines if the customer has to be synchronized with Epicor FSA application.

AgingCreditHold.description: Indicates if customer has been placed into an "Aging Hold" status. A "yes" will trigger notification of this condition in Order Entry and Shipping.

AgingCreditHoldDate.description: Date on which the customer was last placed on aging hold. This field is maintained by the system.

AgingCreditHoldSource.description: Indicates how the customer was placed on aging hold.  Valid values are "MANUAL" and "PROCESS".  "MANUAL" means that the user placed the customer on hold.  “PROCESS” means that the Mass Credit Information Update Process places the customer on aging hold.  This field is maintained by the system.

AgingCreditClearUserID.description: The UserFile.DCDUSERID value of the user that last cleared the customer's aging hold. This field is maintained by the system.

AgingCreditClearDate.description: The date that the user last cleared the customer's aging hold. This field is maintained by the system.

AgingCreditCode.description: The aging code assigned to the customer.

ImporterOfRecord.description: Indicates whether this customer is importer of records or not. Used for Avalara Tax Connect calculation.

UseBlindShipping.description: Indicates whether sales orders from this sold to customer should be treated as Blind Shipments by Manifest.

CentralCollection.description: Flag that indicates if the Customer is for Central Collection.

TaxValidationStatus.description: Tax ID Validation Status. Not Validated – 0, Valid – 1, Invalid – 2.

Inactive.description: Indicates if the record is inactive.

ConsolidateMilestoneBilling.description: If selected then within AR Invoice milestone billing it will generate a single AR Invoice header grouped by customer / currency and terms code.

DUNS.description: Data Universal Numbering System. Unique identification number used to check credit history and assess financial health.

CreditHoldReason.description: The reason a customer is placed on credit hold.

CreditHoldNote.description: Optional notes that can be entered when a customer is placed on credit hold

AddrList.description: Customer Address in format delimited string

AdvancedLaborRate.description: This value is a temp field that is populated from JCSyst.AdvancedLaborRate

ApplyHoldToOrders.description: Apply credit hold status to orders

BTAddrList.description: Bill To Address in format delimited list

COASegReferences.description: List of fields which are referenced by COA segments.

ECCPaymentCodes.description: List of available ECC Payment Method codes

ECCPaymentDesc.description: List of available ECC Payment Method descriptions

ECCPaymentList.description: List of ECC Payment Method codes selected

ECCPaymentXDesc.description: List of ECC Payment Method descriptions selected

ECCShipViaList.description: List of ECC Ship Via codes selected

ExtCoExist.description: Indicates if an "Multi" External Company record exists to enable GlobalCust

GlbFlag.description: Indicates if the Customer is Global (master or linked)

GlbLink.description: Delimited list of GlbCompany and GlbCustNum that is linking to this customer

IntegrationFlag.description: Indicates if integrated with a financial package

IntRunChangeCountry.description: Flag used for integrations whether to run the on change country logic.

ListCode.description: Identifies the default price list for the customer.  Must be unique and present in PriceLst table.

LLLBAssetAssess.description: Indicates if the Clients Asset and Income Assessment has been received

NAChildCrdAvail.description: Customer?s credit available to be shared with his Children in National Account

NACreditCust.description: If the customer in the Credit Checking National Account

NACreditUpdated.description: Customer is in Credit National Account and his Credit data have been updated, so recalculation is needed

NAParentCrdAvail.description: Available Parent?s Credit in National Accout

NAPoolCrdAvail.description: Available credit from credit pools to be used by this customer in National account.

NATotalCreditAvail.description: Total Credit Available from National Account

NATotalCreditUsed.description: Total Credit Used from National Account

NettingVendorID.description: A user defined external Netting Vendor ID.  This must be existing Vendor ID within the file.

PIFlag.description: Indicates whether PI fields should be enabled or not

SendShipment.description: flag indicating whether packing slips are sent to integration partner

TotGlbInvoiceCredit.description: Total Global Invoice Credit (including current company)

TotGlbOpenCredit.description: Total Global Open Credit (based on GlbCredInc flags)

TotGlbOrderCredit.description: Total Global Order Credit (including current company)

TotGlbPICredit.description: Total Global Payment Instruments Credit (including current company)

TotOpenCredit.description: Total Credit based on CredInc flags

TotOpenInvoicesLC.description: Total number of open invoices for LCs

TotOpenOrderLC.description: Total number of open orders for LCs

TotOpenPI.description: Total number of open Payment Instruments

ELIRcptDefReportID.description: E-invoice default report id for cash receipt

CurrencyList.description: List of currency id's that can be selected. Options are base currency and the customer currency

CurrencyIDDesc.description: Currency ID Description of the customer currency

CurrencyIDBase.description: Currency ID of the base currency

DocCreditLimit.description: The credit limit in the customer's currency

DocCustPILimit.description: The PI Credit Limit in the customer's currency

DocTotLCCredit.description: Open Letter of Credit in customer's currency

DocTotLCOpenOrders.description: Open Order Credit against Letter of Credit in customer's currency

DocTotLCCumInvoices.description: Cumulative Invoice Amount against Letter of Credit in customer's currency

DocTotLCInvoiceBal.description: Open Invoice Balance against Letter of Credit in customer's currency

DocTotLCUsed.description: Total LC/ECG Used in customer's currency

DocTotInvoiceCredit.description: Total Invoice Credit in the customer's currency

DocTotPICredit.description: Payment Instrument Credit in customer's currency

DocTotGlbInvoiceCredit.description: Total Global Invoice Credit (including current company) in the customer's currency

DocTotGlbOrderCredit.description: Total Global Order Credit (including current company) in the customer's currency

DocTotGlbPICredit.description: Total Global Payment Instruments Credit (including current company) in the customer's currency

DocTotGlbOpenCredit.description: Total Global Open Credit (based on GlbCredInc flags) in the customer's currency

DocTotOpenLC.description: Open Orders against Letters of Credit in customer's currency

DocTotOpenOrderLC.description: Open Order Credit against Letters of Credit in customer's currency

DocTotOpenInvoicesLC.description: Open Invoices against Letters of Credit in customer's currency

CustNum.description: A  unique integer assigned by the system to new customers by the customer maintenance program.

ShipToNum.description: This is the ShipToNum of the Customer Ship To.

SeqNum.description: This is the sequence or hierarchy of the price list codes associated with the ship to.  The lower the number the higher the priority is in the hierarchy.

ListCode.description: Unique set of characters entered by the user to identify the Price List master within the company.

CustID.description: A user defined external customer ID.  This must be unique within the file.  This ID may be used in certain screen displays or reports where a full customer name is inappropriate. Therefore users should use meaningful characters as they would in any other master file. This master file key is a little different in that the user can change. This change is allowed because the system is not using the CustID as a foreign key in any other file.  Rather it uses the CustNum field which is assigned to the customer by the system.

CustNum.description: A  unique integer assigned by the system to new customers by the customer maintenance program.  This field is used as the foreign key to identify the customer in other files such as OrderHed or InvcHead.  The end user should never see this field in the application but can use it for reporting purposes.

Name.description: The full name of the customer.

City.description: The city portion of the customer's main address.

State.description: The state or province portion of the customer's main address.

Zip.description: The zip or postal code portion of the customer's main address.

Country.description: The country of the main customer address.

ResaleID.description: Optional field used to record the customer's State Tax Identification number, which is displayed on Sales Acknowledgments.

TerritoryID.description: The SalesTer.TerritoryID value of the territory assigned to the customer.

ShipToNum.description: Contains the key of the default ship to for the customer. A blank value indicates that the name and address in the Customer file is considered the default ship to. This field is updated when the user marks the check box in ship to maintenance indicating that the ship to is to be designated as the default. This default will be used in areas such as Sales Order entry.

TermsCode.description:
The Terms.TermsCode value of the default sales terms associated with the customer. A default may be supplied by XaSyst.TermsCode if not blank. The terms will default into quotes and orders for this customer.
For invoices not related to a sales order, these terms will also default into the invoice.

CreditHold.description: Indicates if customer has been placed into a "Credit Hold" status. A "yes" will trigger notification of this condition in Order Entry and Shipping.

GroupCode.description: Contains the CustGrup.GroupCode value of the customer group that the customer has been assigned to. This field is used by the application for sorting or filtering on reports and can also be associated with price lists.

PhoneNum.description: The general Business Phone Number for the customer. Displayed in Order entry when no contact is given or when contact has a blank phone number.

CountryNum.description: Contains the Country.CountryNum value of the country the customer is located in.

CustomerType.description: Used to define the type of the customer record.

NoContact.description: Indicates whether or not this customer will be included in marketing lists.

AllowShipTo3.description: When yes, a ShipTo CustID on certain forms will be enabled. This allows a shipto of a different customer to be referenced as a 3rd party for a document.

CustPartOpts.description:
Determines print options for any customer parts related to this customer.

M = Main Part Reference (Default)
S = Secondary Part Reference
O = Only Part Reference Printed
N = Customer Part Not Printed

HasBank.description: If yes, indicates that Customer has at least one associated CustBank record.

ShipToNum.description: The ID assigned by the user which makes this record unique for the customer.  When a customer is created a ShipTo record is automatically created by the system for that customer with a ShipToNum equal to NULL.

Name.description: Name of the ShipTo. When creating new records the Customer.Name is used as a default.

Address1.description: The first line of the ShipTo address.

Address2.description: The second line of the ShipTo address.

Address3.description: The third line of the ShipTo address.

City.description: The city portion of the ShipTo address.

State.description: The state or province portion of the ShipTo address.

ZIP.description: The zip or postal code portion of the ShipTo address.

Country.description: The country portion of the ShipTo address.

ResaleID.description: The State Tax Identification Number. Used in Order Entry and prints on Sales Acknowledgements.

SalesRepCode.description: The SalesRep.SalesRepCode value of the default salesperson for the customer. Used as a default in Order Entry and Invoice entry. The SalesRep from the customer master is used as an initial default when creating new ship to.

TerritoryID.description: The SalesTer.TerritoryID value of the territory the customer is assigned to.

ShipViaCode.description: The ShipVia.ShipViaCode value of the default ShipVia assigned to the customer. Used as a default in Order Entry, Shipping and Invoicing.  The shipvia from the customer record for this shipto is used as the initial default when creating new ShipTo records.

PrimSCon.description: The CustCnt.ConNum of the default shipping contact for the ShipTo location. The primary shipping contact is used as a default in the shipping process.

FaxNum.description: The fax number for the ShipTo location. isplayed in Order entry when no shipping contact is given for or when contact has a blank phone number.

PhoneNum.description: The business phone number for the ShipTo location. Displayed in Order entry when no shipping contact is given for or when contact has a blank phone number.

TaxExempt.description: Determines whether or not the ShipTo location is normally exempt from sales tax. Used as a default in invoice entry.  If the field is non-blank it is considered exempt.   This code is totally user definable and no validation is required.

EDIShipNum.description: A mutually agreed upon value that links a customer's EDI shipto record (an N1 / ST) to the Manufacturing System DB ShipTo record.

CountryNum.description: The Country.CountryNum value of the country selected in the ShipTo.Country field.

LangNameID.description: The LangName.LangNameID value of the default Language assigned to the ShipTo location. This controls which language will be selected when extracting part descriptions from PartLangDesc table.

BorderCrossing.description: Area/city code from where goods cross the border. This field is intended for Intrastat reporting. The field can be blank to indicate the value from the Customer table. This field is only visible if ISSyst.EnableHarbour is set.

FormatStr.description: Optional custom address format for the ShipTo location.

TaxRegionCode.description: Contains the TaxRgn.TaxRegionCode value of the customer's tax region for purposes of Sales Tax calculations.

EMailAddress.description: The email address of the ShipTo location.

TerritorySelect.description:
Determines how the ShipTo location should be assigned to a territory. There are 3 methods for Territory assignment in Ship-to:

Sync - Keep the ShipTo territory synchronized with the territory on the
       main customer record. (ShipTo.TerritoryID = Customer.TerritoryID)
Syst - Let the system (Get Territory) determine the territory to assign         to the ShipTo based on territory boundaries.
Lock - Check this method after assigning the territory manually to 
       prevent the system from attempting to reassign the territory.

PendingTerritoryID.description: The pending sales territory that the customer will be assigned to based on changes to the territory boundaries.  This functionality is only available with the CRM module.

CreatedByEDI.description: Determines whether or not the ShipTo record was created by an EDI transaction.

ExternalID.description: Unique identifier of the ShipTo from an external General Ledger interface such as the EuroFinancial integration.

TaxAuthorityCode.description: The TaxAuthorityCd.TaxAuthorityCode value of the Tax Authority assigned to this ShipTo location.

GlobalLock.description: Disable this record from receiving global updates.

EDICode.description: The Trading Partner ID that is used for incoming and outgoing EDI transactions.

DemandDeliveryDays.description: Days to use in calculating the Order Detail Ship By date from the incoming need by date.

DemandDateType.description:
Indicates incoming date type.  Values are:
S - Shipping Date
N - Need By Date

DemandAddLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when adding a new order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandAddAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the add lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandChangeLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when changing an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.  This does not include changes to quantity or dates.

DemandChangeAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the change lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandCancelLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when canceling an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandCancelAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the cancel lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandNewLineLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when adding a new order line record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandNewLineAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the new line lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandQtyChangeLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when changing the quantity on an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandQtyChangeAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the quantity change lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

DemandChangeDateLeadTime.description: The number of days from today to give a warning when changing the date on an order release record from an incoming shipping schedule.

DemandChangeDateAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the change date lead time is breeched.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the lead time has been breeched but allow the record to be accepted).

CODCheck.description: Cashier's Check or Money order is required on COD Delivery

CODAmount.description: Amount due on Cashier's check or money order

GroundType.description: Valid Values are blank, "Any" (Any Payment), "GF" (Guaranteed Funds), or "Cash" (Currency)

NotifyFlag.description: Indicates whether to send an email notification of delivery

NotifyEMail.description: The list of email address to notify about a delivery

DeclaredIns.description: Flag to indicate that an insurance value was declared on delivery

PeriodicityCode.description: Periodicity Code.  Must be a valid code in the Periodicity table.

ServRelease.description: Service Signature release is on file

CommercialInvoice.description: Added for international shipping, Is a commercial invoice required

ShipExprtDeclartn.description: Added for international shipping. Shipper's Export Declaration required

CertOfOrigin.description: For International shipping.  Certificate of Orgin required.

LetterOfInstr.description: For International shipping.  Shipper's Letter of Instruction.

OverrideCarrier.description: Override Carrier Defaults.  If not checked then the customer values will be used if overriden else the Site values

OverrideService.description: Override Service Options.  If not checked then the customer values will be used if overriden else the Site values

DemandUseCustomerValues.description: Indicates if the demand fields from the customer should be used.

EarlyBuffer.description: Used to calculate on-time delivery performance rating

LateBuffer.description: Used to calculate on-time delivery performance rating

DemandUnitPriceDiff.description: Indicates if the unit price between the demand and the contract should be validated.  If this flag is checked, and the prices are different, when the demand is accepted a record will be written to the DemandLog table.

DemandUnitPriceDiffAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the unit price between the demand and the contract is different.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the unit price is different but allow the record to be accepted).

ExcFromVal.description: A flag that indicates whether this address should be validated by the tax service.

AddressVal.description: A flag indicating that an address has already been validated. This helps improve the performance of the bulk address validation process by allowing address that have already been validated to be skipped. This flag is set anytime a successful validation is performed, either by the bulk address validation or validation from the Customer form.

DemandCheckForPart.description: Check for the part in the Part master.

DemandCheckForPartAction.description: Indicates what type of action to take if the Check for Part options is selected and the part is not in the part master file.  Options are B (reject the change) or W (warning - alert that the part is not in the part master but allow the record to be accepted).

FFAddress1.description: International Shipping. The first line of the Frieght Forwarder main address.

FFAddress2.description: International Shipping. The second line of the Frieght Forwarder main address.

FFCity.description: Shipping, The city portion of the Frieght Forwarder main address.

FFState.description: International Shipping. The state or province portion of the shipment Frieght Forwarder main address.

FFCompName.description: International Shipping. Frieght Forwarder company name

FFPhoneNum.description: International Shipping. Frieght Forwarder Phone number

ChangedBy.description: Userid of user who made the last change to this record.

ChangeDate.description: The date that the record was last changed

ChangeTime.description: The time that the record was last change (seconds since midnight)

DeliveryConf.description:
Determines the level of delivery confirmation.
1 - No Signature Required
2 - Adult Signature Required
3 - Confirmation Required
4 - Verbal Confirmation Required

FFAddress3.description: Freight Forwarder Country portion of the address

ETCAddrChg.description: Used to determine if an address changed because of the tax integration.  If true, the tax integration changed the address.

IsAlternate.description: Indicates if this is a alternate ShipTo. An alternate is a Shipto that is valid for this customer, but is defined/maintained by the "master" customer. See ShipTo.MasterCustNum/MasterShiptoNum.

MasterCustNum.description: Pertains to Alternate Shipto (IsAlternate). Contains the CustNum of the Shipto record that is the "Master". Changes made to the Master, are replicated to the alternates.

MasterShipToNum.description: Pertains to Alternate Shipto (IsAlternate). Contains the ShipToNum of the Shipto record that is the "Master". Changes made to the Master, are replicated to the alternates.

DemandCheckShipAction.description: Check Partial Shipments Action: B =Stop  and W = Warning

DemandCloseRejSkd.description: Define if at the moment of processing a demand the process should also close those rejected schedules that remain at demand entry

CheckDateCapPromise.description: If this flags is turned on then Demand CTP will automatically be executed as a part of the demand to order process.

CheckConfirmCapPromise.description: Confirm or not the Capable to Promise jobs from Demand Entry

CheckUpdateCapPromise.description: If checked, Updates the date in Demand Entry

DemandCapPromiseDate.description: This field will define the dates that will be validated as a part of the demand to order process. The valid values for this combo will be:  Need By (N), Ship By (S) and Both (B)

DemandCapPromiseAction.description: The value on this field will define the action to be taken when validating CTP. The valid values for this combo box will be: Warning (W) and Stop (S)

DemandCapPromiseUpdate.description:
This field will define the dates that will be updated as a part of the demand to order process. The valid options will be:
Need By (N), Ship By (S) and Both  (B) NOTE .-In all the cases above the update of date will only be done if the CTP dates are beyond the dates on the file.

OTSmartString.description: When set to TRUE the smart string functionality will only be processed when the incoming demand is new. After it has been processed and saved, if a retransmission is sent the smart string values will be ignored.

DemandCheckConfig.description: If true then demand will be rejected when one or more demand lines are not configured properly. Applies only to Configurable parts.

DemandCheckCfgAction.description: Indicates the action to be taken if configuration values have not been entered for one or more demand lines.

PayTypeDesc.description:
Describes the billing type.  Valid values and their description are:
1 - Shipper
2 - FedEx  Collect
3 - Third Party
4 - UPS Prepaid
5 - FedEx Recipient
6 - UPS Consignee
7 - UPS Freight Collect
8 - UPS Free On Board
9 - UPS Cost and Freight
10 - UPS Delivery Duty Paid
11 - UPS Shpping Duty and Tax Consignee

RelatedToFile.description:
Identifies the master file to which the GL Control is related to.  This field is used to properly isolate controls to the masters they are related to.
For example; Customer, PartClass identifies controls that are related to Customers and Part Classes

Key1.description: Major component of the foreign key of the related master record. For example: For a "Part"  control this field would contain the related Part Number,  for a "Customer"  it contains the Customer.CustNum.

Key2.description:
2nd component of the foreign key to the related master record.
The usage of this field is dependent on the type of record.

TaxCode.description: Descriptive code assigned by user which uniquely identifies a Sales Tax master record.  Can't be blank. This is used as a foreign key in other files and may be used in displays/reports where space for the full description is limited.
